sn35.99:1.2: A concentrated monk discerns things as they have come to be.
sn35.99:1.3: And what does he discern as it has come to be?
sn35.99:1.4: “He discerns, as it has come to be, that ‘The eye is inconstant’ …
sn35.99:1.8: ‘Whatever arises in dependence on eye-contact—experienced either as pleasure, as pain, or as neither-pleasure-nor-pain—that too is inconstant.’
sn35.99:1.9: “He discerns, as it has come to be, that ‘The intellect is inconstant’ …
sn35.99:1.13: ‘Whatever arises in dependence on intellect-contact—experienced either as pleasure, as pain, or as neither-pleasure-nor-pain—that too is inconstant.’
sn35.99:1.15: A concentrated monk discerns things as they have come to be.”
